titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Yale and New Haven Audio and Visual Recordings
Yale and New Haven audio and visual recordings
titlestmt.author: compiled by Daniel Hartwig

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday March 14, 2020 at 07:23
Finding aid revision description not supplied.

did.unittitle: Yale and New Haven audio and visual recordings
did.abstract: The materials consist of motion pictures and audiorecordings documenting Yale. Included are films of news coverage and interviews, class reunions, commencements, presidential inaugurations, football games, and other events and activities at Yale.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Forms part of Yale Record Group 48-B (YRG 48-B), Yale audiovisual records and collections.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy for their personal use should consult Copying Services information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.
Copies of commercially produced audiovisual materials contained in this collection cannot be made for researcher use outside of the repository.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Yale and New Haven Audio and Visual Recordings (RU 803). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The records are arranged by accession.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The materials consist of motion pictures and audiorecordings documenting Yale. Included are films of news coverage and interviews, class reunions, commencements, presidential inaugurations, football games, and other events and activities at Yale.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: The materials were transferred from Media Services, 2000, Historical Sound Recordings, 2011, and the Office of the President, 2012.

did.unittitle: Audio-visual materials documenting Yale and New Haven
Speaking Freely, Kingman Brewster interviewed by Edwin Newman [commercially-produced]
Kingman Brewster on the Today Show [commercially-produced]
Action Newsmakers, WTNH-TV 8 interview with Kingman Brewster [commercially-produced]
Morton Dean interview of Kingman Brewster [commercially-produced]
Kingman Brewster guest comment on CBS Morning News [commercially-produced]
Kingman Brewster guest comment on CBS News [commercially-produced]
Kingman Brewster guest comment on CBS Morning News [commercially-produced]
Kingman Brewster guest comment on CBS Morning News [commercially-produced]
Kingman Brewster guest comment on CBS Morning News [commercially-produced]
Kingman Brewster, "Independent Sector," guest comment on CBS Morning News [commercially-produced]
Kingman Brewster guest comment on CBS Morning News [commercially-produced]
Kingman Brewster guest comment on CBS Morning News [commercially-produced]
Kingman Brewster and president David Saxon, University of California, on MacNeil/Lehrer Report [commercially-produced]
Kingman Brewster, guest comment, CBS Morning News [commercially-produced]
University of Oregon centennial, keynote lecture by Kingman Brewster, commercially-produced videotape (helical scan) [Â½", 7 Â½ IPS]
"A Conversation with A. Bartlett Giamatti," CPTV commerically-produced videotape (U-matic"[56:14]
A. Bartlett Giamatti on the Dick Cavett Show [commercially-produced]
Bob Nouman conversation with A. Bartlett Giamatti
World News Saturday , Gandolf/A. Bartlett Giamatti, commercially-produced videotape (VHS)
NBC Nightly News , A. Bartlett Giamatti, commercially-produced videotape (VHS)
Energy: The Oil Shortage. What's Going On?
Whatever Happened to the Energy Crisis?
C. Vann Woodward and Guido Calabresi, "Impeachment Hearings: What to Watch For," WTNH-TV special [commercially-produced]
The Inquiring Mind: Science at Yale
PM Magazine with a Yale segment
"A Process of Understanding: The Social Sciences at Yale"
Yale-Dr. Of Letters Award/Smoky Joe Wood Eyewitness News (WFSB-TV Hartford), Action News Eight (WTNH-TV New Haven), Thirty News (WVIT-TV Hartford)[commercially-produced]
Yale Literary Magazine , CBS 60 Minutes , commercially-produced videotape (VHS)
Associate Professor Roman Kuc, Electrical Engineering, demonstration of robotics during halftime of Yale-Cornell Game on PBS, commercially-produced videotape (VHS)
"Bass Donation to Yale," Noon Report (WDFW-TV/Dallas), commercially-produced videotape (VHS)
Yale volunteers and the wider parish, a film by T.W. Peacocke [commercially-produced]
'Neath the Elms, Charles Ives "The Alcotts," 2 audiotapes (reel-to-reel)
Yale footage: Yale vs. Cornell (soccer); Yale vs. Holy Cross (field hockey); Margaret Wynne interview; Yale Bowl scenes; commercially-produced videotape (Helical scan) [1"]
Interviews with Andrea Metkus?, Will Carlin; R.T. Blass footage; commercially-produced videotape (Helical scan) [1"]
President George Bush, Yale University, Lifetime of Leadership Award [commercially-produced]
CBS Sunday Morning feature on Cole Porter, commercially-produced videotape (UVHS)
Yale Commons, Yale Gym, John Lee dinner
David Suskind Show , "God, Man and War at Yale"
Face the Nation , Kingman Brester, 2 film reels (16mm, sound only)
Experimental Film , hosted by John Hollander (includes short film of Stacy Keach)

title.part: A Personal Engagement with Howard Weaver

odd.head: General
odd.p: Videotape of this performance was discovered to have been erased prior to digitization. It has been de-accessioned from this collection as of 8/30/2019.

did.unittitle: Motion picture films documenting Yale University

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color, sound, faded, scenes of campus and possibly commencement

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Heavy fading

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color, sound. Heavy fading.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color, sound.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color, sound, produced by the audio visual center for the alumni board, "color safety film," wrapping, narrated by Norman S. Buck III, includes scenes of campus.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black and white with sound. Begins with speech and cuts to crowd of people.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black and white, sound; includes scenes of various speeches.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black and white, sound; includes various shots of old campus and speeches, old campus, lead states "sleeping giant."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Faded color with sound; includes scenes of campus.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black and white. No sound. Shots from a football game. Dupont safety film. Unclear who is playing Yale.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black and white. No sound. The film is of a football game. Unclear who Yale is playing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color, no sound; film opens with scenes of campus, and an unidentified event being held outside of Sterling Memorial Library (possibly commencement). Procession leaves Sterling Memorial Library with men in uniform.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Faded color with sound; includes series of shots with students.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Faded color with sound. Film slightly damaged (warping).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Faded color with sound.

title.part: Three Who Visited Yale: The Chubb Fellowship

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color with sound; includes series of individuals giving lectures that are interrupted by two individuals. Introduces Sam J. Irwin, Rev. Jesse Jackson, and Elliot Richardson.

title.part: Three Who Visited Yale: The Chubb Fellowship

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color with sound; same film as box 16 (superior condition).

title.part: Haeceity: A Film of Life at Yale

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Film damaged

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Film damaged

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color with sound, produced by Philip Garvin; introduction gives credit to alumni of 1968; campus scenes with narrations.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color (heavy fading) with sound; film by Philip Garvin, includes scenes of Yale with interviews.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black and white.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black and white with sound. Box #23 is the same film as box #25, but in poorer condition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black and white, sound.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black and white with sound; Griswold speaks with shots of Yale cut in (both historical and current). The film is presented by the alumni board.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color with sound. Film leader titled: "News Reel Yale 1954." Film opens with shots of Yale buildings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color, sound.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color with sound; produced by Herbert Scopal.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black and white with sound. Film opens with commencement scenes throughout campus. Film also shows various artifacts (vases, statues, etc…), including manuscripts and books. Film also shows "science building nears completion." Film encompasses many aspects of Yale.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black and white (heavy fading), no sound; Scenes of men in lab coats dining (possibly participating in an event) with a band in the background.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black and white with sound; film about the personal "impressions of two Yale alumni at the 1969 class reunion" (intro slides). Film has sets of interviews.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black and white, sound.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black and white with sound. Film opens with shots of a man giving a speech, shots pan to the audience. Also includes various interviews.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black and white, sound.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black and white, sound.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black and white, sound. Film opens with man speaking to camera and transitions to shots of campus with interviews. Additonal scenes include outdoor events and a football game.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black and white, color. A shorter version of films in boxes 36 and 37.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black and white with sound; shots of various football games. Individuals speak into the camera, which divides the shot of football games.

title.part: For God, for Country, and for Yale: Yale Class of 1939 - 25 Years Out!

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color with no sound. Includes scenes of men playing golf and an outdoor reception.

title.part: From the Outside; "Skin of our Teeth"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contents and condition are unknown

title.part: Co-Education: The Year they Liberated Yale

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color with sound; Various shots of people speaking and women being interviewed. Includes scenes of Yale playing a football game possibly against Cornell and shots of campus buildings.

did.unittitle: From the College Campus - Yale (electric music)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black and white with sound; introduction names Yale University, Trinity College, St. Joseph College, and Wesleyan University. Film opens with a shots of Woolsey Hall's exterior and then shows a concert.

title.part: From the College Campus: Thinking Man's Game

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color with sound; scenes of students around campus. Shots of the New Haven green and New Haven. Film also includes interviews with students. Title card reads: "Reflections from Yale."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black and white with sound; Yale playing Trinity College, St. Joseph College and Wesleyan University. Scenes of the games are interrupted by interviews which comprise the vast majority of the film.

title.part: Football at Yale: A Story of Tradition

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black and white with sound; opens with scenes of the Yale bowl and includes shots of individuals and groups. The lead film indicates that it is part II.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fading color with sound; film opens at the Yale bowl and includes interviews with people with cuts into football games. The teams Yale played against are presented.

title.part: Football: Yale vs. Brown, Yale vs. Cornell, Yale vs. Dartmouth

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black and white with sound; narrated by Jordan Oliver and produced by the Yale Alumni Board.

title.part: Brewster on CBS's Face the Nation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black and white with sound. Interview with Kingman Brewster.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fading color with sound; film opens with a pamphlet stating: "Symposium: the philosophy of Paul Weiss, The catholic university of America." Film cuts to a speaker and includes various scenes with Paul Weiss in multiple locations.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color with sound; presented by the Yale University Alumni Board. Includes various shots of campus libraries both interior and exterior with tours of some of the libraries rooms and collections.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color with sound; film opens with a librarian speaking and includes various shots of individuals inside Sterling Memorial Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color with sound; same film as boxes 50 and 51 but lacks the introductory speech at the beginning from the librarian.

did.physdesc: 16mm Kodachrome print with optical soundtrack. Film stock dates to 1953. There is some residue in the image area near the head of the print. Approximately 3 cement splices throughout the body of the print. Good condition.
16 mm Kodachrome print with optical soundtrack. Film stock dates to 1953. This print has good color and few splices (approximately 5).

16mm Kodachrome print with optical soundtrack. Film stock dates to 1953. This print has a splice repaired with masking tape at the head, and approximately 4 cement splices throughout the body of the print. There is sprocket damage toward the end, but it has been repaired. The color is fairly good.

16mm Kodachrome print with optical soundtrack. Film stock dates to 1953. This print is incomplete, with some of the beginning title sequence missing. There are a few torn sprockets and a tear at the head, probably due to projector damage. Some sprockets throughout show damage, but have been repaired. This print has quite a few splices (more than 10). Of the four prints of this film, this print is in the worst shape.

title.part: Yale Political Union Presents: Bob Guccione

did.unittitle: Yale - Yale University #309 , directed by M. Lerner
Yale 1931: 25th Reunion , Fred Loesser, Class of 1931, Saybrook

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black and white with no sound (warped); includes a collection of nostalgic scenes presented by the 15th Reunion Committee of 1931. Also includes scenes of campus life and buildings.

title.part: What About Yale: Reflections from Yale

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color with sound (good condition); title card reads: "Reflections from Yale." Produced by Warren Wallace.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color with sound (slight warping). Produced by Vision Associates. Various images of nature, buildings, still life, sterling silver dishes, and table wear shots.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color with no sound (good condition). Footage of Archaeological dig sites including W.M. Strongback.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color with sound (good condition); includes scenes of campus buildings and campus life.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Faded color with sound (good condition). A visit to Wilmarth S. Lewis and Horace Walpole at Farmington. Includes interviews and footage of artwork and writings.

did.unittitle: The Off Season , written and directed by Steve Starosta

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black and white video, 28 minutes.

did.unittitle: Grow Your Own Beard , by Ziya Ozkahyaoglu

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color with sound (good condition). Various footage from nature; waterfalls, mushrooms, landscapes, statues, and butterflies.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color with sound (good condition). Appears to be a light show or light demonstration (substances under a microscope) from the Yale department of biophysics and biochemistry.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color with sound (warped). Footage of hands, in varying shapes and positions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Faded color with sound (warped). Animated Asian drawings with occasional cuts to interviews. Produced by I-Cheng Loh.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color with no sound (heavily warped, poor condition); Footage of members of the Class of 1922 including Truman Gardner, "Hett" Hettington, Blondy Robbins, Yates Smith, "Mac" Aldrich and Larry Kelly. Includes scenes of class dinner.

title.part: Yale Class of 1922: A Pictorial Record of the Quindecennial Reunion of the Class of 1922

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color with no sound (poor condition). Scenes of campus buildings and reunion footage.

title.part: Dr. R.V. Jones and Dr. McCann

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes semi-nude students.

title.part: Yale '74: Summer School of Music and Art...in Norfolk

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is a one hour discussion with four speakers, including Alexander Bickel and Abraham S. Goldstein.

did.unittitle: Damn Yalies , a gift of John Babich, Class of '84, film from the 2nd Year Show

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color with no sound (heavy damage, poor condition)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes two audiorecordings of the same content, although one audiorecording is a few minutes longer than the other.

did.unittitle: Standish Lawder - Lecture on Dada and Surrealism
Standish Lawder - Lecture on Dada and Surrealism 1 of 2
Standish Lawder - Lecture on Dada and Surrealism 2 of 2
Michel Beaujour - The Philosophy of Dadaism
Michel Beaujour - The Philosophy of Dadaism 1 of 2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The container has 2 dates listed, May 19, 1973 and May 19, 1974. The content confirms the correct date as May 19, 1974.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy for their personal use should consult Copying Services information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

did.unittitle: A. Bartlett Giamatti Memorial with the Whiffenpoofs
Motion picture film documenting Yale University

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy for their personal use should consult Copying Services information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Film taken by Frederic William Porter, Class of 1932. Film shows scenes of old campus and the area around York and Wall Streets where the contruction site of Sterling Memorial Library is seen. Film also shows an unidentified crew race. Film runs just over 12 minutes, b/w with no sound.

did.unittitle: Film by Frederic William Porter, Class of 1932.
